label: organism
Notes: Notes extracted from retired sheet: is about ("organism" has role "pathogen role"); OR should it be as broad as it's label: is about "organism". Also, do we use "organism" or is "is about organism"? is about OBI:0100026 organism if we have "host organism name" then this equivalent would be "pathogen organism name" or "organism name". In CANCOGEN this is pathogen organism. Broad "organism" label but we usually use this as pathogen organism. Rhiannon's concern is that on the ontology end, the ID should actually point to "pathogen organism name" which would be a subclass of "organism name" and sibbling class to "host organism name" It's looking like we will always have "data item" values but they semantically describe non-data and data classes to address the subjects and non-data related relations are applicable then.
LinkML:class_name: AMBR | B2B2B | CanCOGeNCovid19 | GRDI | HPAI | HPAIEnviro | HPAIFood | HPAIHost | HPAIWW | Influenza | Measles | Mpox | MpoxInternational | NMLPathogenAgnostic | PathogenAgnostic | PHA4GE | WastewaterAMR | WastewaterPathogenAgnostic | WastewaterSARS-CoV-2
CATEGORY (TEMP): anatomical entity > material anatomical entity > organism
definition: A data field which describes the taxonomic name of the organism.
user interface definition: PCDS: The scientific name of the organism or taxon that is the focus of the study, assay, or diagnostic test.
comment: PCDS: The target taxonomic name refers to the specific species, genus, or other taxonomic classification that the assay or diagnostic test is designed to identify, detect, or characterize. This may include the full binomial name (Genus species) or a higher taxonomic rank (e.g., genus, family) when species-level identification is not applicable. Avoid abbreviations. Search for taxonomic names here: ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy.

comment: PCDS: Provide the name of the breed of the host organism.

user interface definition: PCDS: The stage of the infectious disease at the time of sampling.

These stages describe the progression from initial exposure to pathogen to recovery or resolution. Select the infectious disease stage from the pick list provided.

label: signs and symptoms
Notes: Notes extracted from retired sheet: Should we specify these as host fields? is about GENEPIO:0100291 signs and symptoms at time of specimen collection
LinkML:class_name: CanCOGeNCovid19 | HPAI | HPAIHost | Measles | Mpox | MpoxInternational | PHA4GE
definition: A data field which describes the perceived change in function or sensation (loss, disturbance or appearance) indicative of a disease, reported by a patient.
user interface definition: PCDS: A perceived change in function or sensation, (loss, disturbance or appearance) indicative of a disease, reported by a patient.
comment: PCDS: Select all of the symptoms experienced by the host from the pick list.
editor note: Generally, "symptoms" are subjective and reported by the patient, while "signs" are observed and reported by a clinician. However, data collected using case report forms are usually relayed by the patient and thus both are deamed "reported by a patient" in this definition.

label: variant designation
Notes: Notes extracted from retired sheet: is about GENEPIO:0100293 variant designation
LinkML:class_name: CanCOGeNCovid19 | PHA4GE
definition: A data field which describes the variant classification of the lineage/clade.
user interface definition: PCDS: The variant classification of the lineage/clade i.e. variant, variant of concern.
comment: PCDS: If the lineage/clade is considered a Variant of Concern, select Variant of Concern from the pick list. If the lineage/clade contains mutations of concern (mutations that increase transmission, clincal severity, or other epidemiological fa ctors) but it not a global Variant of Concern, select Variant. If the lineage/clade does not contain mutations of concern, leave blank.

label: experimental specimen role type

LinkML:class_name: B2B2B | GRDI | HPAI | HPAIEnviro | HPAIFood | HPAIHost | HPAIWW | Measles | Mpox | MpoxInternational | WastewaterAMR | WastewaterPathogenAgnostic | WastewaterSARS-CoV-2
definition: A data field which describes the type of control used in the experiment.
user interface definition: PCDS: The type of role that the sample represents in the experiment.
comment: PCDS: Samples can play different types of roles in experiments. A sample under study in one experiment may act as a control or be a replicate of another sample in another experiment. This field is used to distinguish samples under study from controls, replicates, etc. If the sample acted as an experimental control or a replicate, select a role type from the picklist. If the sample was not a control, leave blank or select 'Not Applicable'.

label: prevalence metrics
LinkML:class_name: GRDI | HPAI | HPAIEnviro | HPAIFood | HPAIHost | HPAIWW
definition: A data field which describes metrics regarding the prevalence of the pathogen of interest obtained from a surveillance project.
user interface definition: PCDS: Metrics regarding the prevalence of the pathogen of interest obtained from a surveillance project.
comment: PCDS: Risk assessment requires detailed information regarding the quantities of a pathogen in a specified location, commodity, or environment. As such, it is useful for risk assessors to know what types of information are available through documented methods and results. Provide the metric types that are available in the surveillance project sample plan by selecting them from the pick list. The metrics of interest are ' Number of total samples collected', 'Number of positive samples', 'Average count of hazard organism', 'Average count of indicator organism'. You do not need to provide the actual values, just indicate that the information is available.
example of usage: PCDS: Number of total samples collected, Number of positive samples

It is recommended that the IRIDA sample name be the same as, or contain, the specimen collector sample ID for better traceability. It is also recommended that the IRIDA sample name mirror the GISAID accession. IRIDA sample names cannot contain slashes. Slashes should be replaced by underscores.
